prompt_identifiers.concept: UploadtoDiscordandgettheURLoftheuploadedimage You can easily get a URL from

metadata.original_prompt: UploadtoDiscordandgettheURLoftheuploadedimage. You can easily get a URL from any image when browsing the internet (including the ones inside Discord) by right clicking the mouse button and selecting the option “copy image address” . To upload you can simple paste in the message field or drag and drop any picture from your computer. We usually add the URL(s) before the text prompt: http:/web.com/image01.jpgmy text prompt here You can add multiple URLs to help you achieve your goals, like the example below: http:/web.com/image01.jpg http:/web.com/image02.jpgpink panther as human photo realistic unreal engine raytracing cartoon style --ar 2:3 + + = = /imagine /imagine
metadata.source_file: Midjourney Prompt Master imagine (Complete Illustrated Guide) (Daniel Farjoun) (z-library.sk, 1lib.sk, z-lib.sk).txt

prompt_identifiers.concept: UploadtoDiscordandgettheURLoftheuploadedimage An image prompt could look like these

Example with Input Weight: /imagine prompt: http://imageurl.jpgA forest above a creek --iw [x] [X] is the weight in numbers like the examples below: --iw INPUT IMAGE --iw -1 --iw 0.5 --iw 2 --iw -0.5 --iw 0.75--iw 0.25 --iw 1 IMPORTANT: Image Weight prompts won’tworkinversion4 . You should add --v 3 to enable version 3.
